MINI MINUTES FOR APR-02-2021 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, LAINTIFFS ELEASAR GENOVE AND JONAS MARZAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND-AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DENIAL OF LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IS RARELY JUSTIFIED. (MORGAN V. SUP. CT. (1959) 172 CAL.APP.2D 527, 530.) ORDINARILY THE VALIDITY OF AN AMENDED PLEADING WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED AT THIS STAGE; IT MAY BE SUBJECT TO LATER MOTIONS. (KITTREDGE SPORTS CO. V. SUP. CT. (1989) 213 CAL.APP.3D 1045, 1048.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT ADEL SHAPIRO'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND DISMISS OR STAY CROSS-COMPLAINT: ADELE SHAPIRO'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS PREMATURE. SHAPIRO'S MOTION SEEKS ARBITRATION OF "ALL CLAIMS THAT ARISE FROM, ARE BASED ON, OR ARE INEXTRICABLY INTERTWINED WITH THE BPA." PLAINTIFFS HAVE PENDING A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CLAIMS AGAINST SHAPIRO THAT APPEAR TO BE BASED ON THE "BPA." ONCE THAT MOTION IS RULED UPON, PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS WILL BE FILED OR NOT. THEN SHAPIRO'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION MAY BE APPROPRIATE; IT WOULD BE INEFFICIENT TO ADDRESS ARBITRATION ISSUES PIECEMEAL. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-23-2021 Continued To Nov-15-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Dismiss Or Stay Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT ADEL SHAPIRO'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Dismiss Or Stay Cross-Complaint:

Adele Shapiro's motion to compel arbitration is denied without prejudice as premature. Shapiro's motion seeks arbitration of "all claims that arise from, are based on, or are inextricably intertwined with the BPA." Plaintiffs have pending a motion for leave to file claims against Shapiro that appear to be based on the "BPA." Once that motion is ruled upon, plaintiffs' claims will be filed or not. Then Shapiro's motion to compel arbitration may be appropriate; it would be inefficient to address arbitration issues piecemeal.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS ELEASAR GENOVE and JONAS MARZAN'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint:

Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a second-amended complaint is granted. Denial of leave to file an amended complaint is rarely justified. (Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530.) Ordinarily the validity of an amended pleading will not be considered at this stage; it may be subject to later motions. (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
